reasoning: The hypothesis `p.Prime ∧ p > 2` correctly encodes "odd prime" since 2 is the only even prime. For f = (X+2)^p, by the binomial theorem coeff n = C(p,n)·2^(p-n), which is nonzero for 0 ≤ n ≤ p, so f.support \ {0} = {1, ..., p}. The Lean sum ∑ₙ f.coeff n · X^(n-1) thus equals ∑_{n=1}^p C(p,n)·2^(p-n)·X^(n-1), which equals ((X+2)^p - 2^p)/X in ℤ[X] since (X+2)^p - 2^p = X · ∑_{n=1}^p C(p,n)·2^(p-n)·X^(n-1). The ambient ring Polynomial ℤ matches ℤ[x], and Irreducible is Mathlib's standard irreducibility predicate. The `hf` binding pattern is logically equivalent to directly substituting the polynomial. The formalization is faithful.
